story:
An off-duty LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT who shot VICTIM dead in Northridge on New Year’s Eve was defending himself against an “active shooter,” officials said — as VICTIM friends claimed VICTIM was only celebrating the holiday with gunfire. 

The unidentified LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT confronted VICTIM outside their apartment complex after he heard the sound of “multiple” gunshots from within VICTIM home that seemed to be getting closer and closer, Department of Homeland Security Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin told CBS News. 

The LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT found VICTIM outside on the building’s ground level armed with a long rifle — and fired a shot to try to “disarm” VICTIM when VICTIM didn’t comply, she added. VICTIM returned at least three shots before the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT fatally shot VICTIM, officials said. 

“On December 31, an off-duty LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT bravely responded to an active shooter situation at his apartment complex,” an ICE spokesperson told The Post. 

“In order to protect his life and that of others, he was forced to defensively use his weapon and exchanged gunfire with the shooter,” the spokesperson added. “Fortunately our brave LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT was not injured while protecting his community.” 

The LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT called the LAPD, who arrived and found VICTIM dead. A police report on the deadly clash obtained by Fox 11 said the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT “confronted VICTIM, identified himself as law enforcement, and claims VICTIM fired toward him, prompting the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT to return fire.” 

But VICTIM kin and Black Lives Matters activists pushed back on that narrative at a Sunday vigil at the apartment complex. No details were released about the race of the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT. 

“You don’t just get to murder people, because you don’t like what they’re doing or how they’re celebrating,” Melina Abdullah, co-founder of BLM Los Angeles, told KABC. 

The mourners demanded justice for VICTIM and said they were planning to meet with District Attorney Nathan Hochman Monday. 

“If I could say anything to the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT, it’s that you’re a murderer. You stole my cousin from me. You stole their father from them,” Jsané Tyler, VICTIM cousin told KABC.

story:
VICTIM was a loving mother, a poet and a partner whose family – along with neighbors across VICTIM stunned city and strangers far beyond – is shocked by the circumstances surrounding VICTIM death.

VICTIM was killed when an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT shot into VICTIM vehicle during an encounter Wednesday morning.

VICTIM ex-husband said VICTIM had just dropped off VICTIM 6-year-old son at school when VICTIM encountered the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS, The Associated Press reported.

VICTIM tried to “weaponize VICTIM vehicle” to run over an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT near an ICE vehicle stuck on a snow-lined street before he opened fire, Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said.

State and local officials have strongly disputed claims the shooting, captured on video, was done in self-defense as tensions exploded amid this week’s deployment of some 2,000 LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS as part of the latest surge in the Trump administration’s nationwide immigration enforcement crackdown and following claims of welfare fraud in the Somali immigrant community.

Still, loved ones and leaders paused to remember the newcomer to Minnesota as “an amazing human being.”

“VICTIM was one of the kindest people I’ve ever known. VICTIM was extremely compassionate,” VICTIM mother, Donna Ganger, told the Minnesota Star Tribune. “VICTIM’s taken care of people all VICTIM life … VICTIM was loving, forgiving and affectionate.” 

Born in Colorado, VICTIM moved to Minnesota last year and lived in the Twin Cities with VICTIM partner, the Star Tribune reported, citing Ganger.

A mother of three, Good had two children, ages 15 and 12, from VICTIM first marriage, The Associated Press reported. VICTIM 6-year-old child’s father died in 2023, according to the Star Tribune. “There’s nobody else in his life,” the child’s grandfather told the newspaper.

After spending most of VICTIM life in Colorado, VICTIM briefly moved to Kansas to live with VICTIM parents for a time after VICTIM husband – a military veteran – died, VICTIM father, Tim Ganger, told The Washington Post. 

“VICTIM had a good life, but a hard life,” he said. “VICTIM was a wonderful person.”

VICTIM death hit VICTIM family especially hard because it was VICTIM older sister’s birthday, VICTIM uncle, Robert Ganger, told CNN affiliate KMGH.

CNN has reached out to VICTIM family.

VICTIM southside Minneapolis home still had holiday decorations on the front porch and in the window Thursday afternoon. Signs of support for VICTIM could be seen in windows around the neighborhood, where VICTIM and VICTIM family had settled relatively recently, neighbors said.

VICTIM had a “really sweet family,” one neighbor told CNN, recalling how VICTIM son would sometimes ask to pet their dog.

A few houses down, another neighbor, Clark Hoelscher told CNN, “We saw VICTIM kids’ sidewalk drawings during the summer.”

Hoelscher did not know the family personally but would see them in the neighborhood from time to time.

As Hoelscher spoke about VICTIM, a tear streamed down their face. “I’m a parent, I’ve helped raise five children. I have two kiddos that are my own.”

“I can’t imagine that they came home and VICTIM isn’t gonna be there anymore,” Hoelscher explained.

Hoelscher, who is also a teacher, said ICE’s presence in Minneapolis was already having an effect before Wednesday’s shooting.

“We have students and families who are scared to be at school,” Hoelscher said. “It’s really hard when I have a student who misses school, I’m just scared about what’s happened to them or their family members,” they added.

Another community member stood in front of VICTIM house for much of the day Thursday, greeting anyone who tried to knock on the door and relaying that VICTIM family would release a statement at the appropriate time.

“VICTIM was a very welcomed member of the community,” neighbor Kimmy Hull said of VICTIM.

Hull said she isn’t surprised by the groundswell of organizing efforts and support in the community.

“What happened with (George) Floyd, there’s a lot of hard things that came out of it. But there’s a lot of really good things. One of the best things was it taught a lot of people how to work together. How to come together,” Hull said.

A former neighbor in Kansas City called VICTIM and VICTIM family “lovely,” CNN affiliates KCTV and KMBC reported.

VICTIM is “a neighbor who, you know, is not a terrorist. Not an extremist,” Joan Rose told KMBC. “VICTIM was just a mom who loved VICTIM kids, loved VICTIM spouse.”

On VICTIM social media accounts, VICTIM described VICTIM as a “poet and writer and wife and mom,” according to the AP. VICTIM recently said VICTIM was “experiencing Minneapolis,” posting a pride flag emoji on VICTIM Instagram account.

A Pinterest account with VICTIM name features photos of VICTIM smiling and holding a young child against VICTIM cheek, along with saved posts about tattoos, hairstyles and home decorating. 

VICTIM also was a devoted Christian who took part in youth mission trips to Northern Ireland when VICTIM was younger, VICTIM ex-husband told the AP. VICTIM also loved to sing and participated in a chorus in high school before studying vocal performance in college, he said.

VICTIM attended Old Dominion University in Virginia, graduating in 2020 with an English degree, the school said in a statement.

During VICTIM time at Old Dominion, VICTIM took advanced courses on creative writing and the craft of fiction, according to one of VICTIM professors, Kent Wascom.

VICTIM excelled in VICTIM studies, Wascom told CNN, even though VICTIM was pregnant and working at the same time.

“A lot of times, young writers write about themselves,” Wascom said. But VICTIM wrote about others.

“VICTIM wrote about elderly people, and about people in and from other places, and in circumstances that were unlike VICTIM own,” Wascom added.

In 2020, VICTIM poetry was awarded ODU’s American Academy of Poets Prize, Wascom said. A Facebook post from the school says VICTIM hosted a podcast with VICTIM late second husband, Tim Macklin.

“May VICTIM life be a reminder of what unites us: freedom, love, and peace. My hope is for compassion, healing, and reflection at a time that is becoming one of the darkest and most uncertain periods in our nation’s history,” Old Dominion President Brian O. Hemphill said in the statement.

Hours after the shooting, throngs of neighbors gathered at a vigil near the scene to remember VICTIM and express their outrage at VICTIM killing. The group surrounded a makeshift shrine of flowers and candles and, at one point, chanted VICTIM name.

“Say it once. Say it twice. We will not put up with ICE,” they also chanted. Some carried signs with messages such as: “Killer ICE off our streets.”

Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey, a Democrat, urged residents to remain calm in the wake of “chaos” in the city. 

“This is a moment where all of us in Minneapolis and beyond, we can rise to the occasion,” Frey said. “We can show them who we are. We can show them the kind of courage, bravery, love and compassion that makes Minneapolis and that makes America.”

story:
LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS shot VICTIM in Portland on Thursday during a traffic stop, a day after the fatal shooting of a woman in Minneapolis stoked outrage over the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.

A Homeland Security Department spokeswoman said in a statement that U.S. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were conducting a “targeted vehicle stop,” and that an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT fired a shot after the driver tried to run them over.

The spokeswoman, Tricia McLaughlin, described the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS’ target as an undocumented immigrant and member of Tren de Aragua, a gang with roots in a Venezuelan prison that has been a frequent target of President Trump. She provided no immediate evidence that the person who was targeted was affiliated with the gang.

Bob Day, Portland’s police chief, said at an evening news conference that he had no information on the identity of VICTIM. He said that the federal officials involved in the shooting were no longer on the scene when local LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS arrived.

The police were alerted to the shooting when the injured man called 911, Chief Day said.

The chief’s comments appeared to underscore the limited cooperation between federal and local officials. Chief Day said local officials knew little about the shooting and that the investigation would be led by the F.B.I. “We do not know if this is an immigration-related event,” he said. “We do not know which federal agencies were involved.”

Gov. Tina Kotek of Oregon called for a full and transparent federal investigation. “Trust is essential to maintain community safety and uphold the law,” she said. “LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS at the direction of the Department of Homeland Security are shattering trust. They are destroying day by day what we hold dear.”

The shooting occurred around 2:15 p.m. Pacific near Adventist Health Portland, a hospital and collection of health clinics in the Hazelwood neighborhood, about eight miles from the city center, officials said.

The driver of the vehicle that federal officials fired into drove off after the shooting, local officials said, and VICTIM were found by the police more than two miles away, with gunshot wounds. Emergency medical technicians who rushed VICTIM to hospitals described VICTIM as Spanish speakers in conversations captured by emergency radio broadcasts. The woman had a gunshot wound to the chest, an E.M.T. told a dispatcher. The man was described as having two gunshot wounds.

Elana Pirtle-Guiney, Portland’s City Council president, said during a council meeting Thursday afternoon that she believed VICTIM were still alive, though local officials later said they did not know VICTIM condition.

Teddy Jay, 20, a resident of the Bria apartment complex in Hazelwood, said he believed VICTIM lived in the complex but that he did not know VICTIM names. He said he saw VICTIM arrive by truck earlier in the afternoon.

“Basically, I was sitting in the house. I heard complete chaos. A whole lot of police sirens,” he said.

Mr. Jay said there was a bloody footprint on the sidewalk outside his building. He said he later saw an official providing medical aid and loading VICTIM into an ambulance.

Residents said the neighborhood has a high crime rate. A former resident at the complex, Debbie Rembert, 60, said the area “was so horrible — the shootings and the robberies and the drugs.”

Portland was the site of months of clashes between demonstrators and law enforcement after the murder of George Floyd by Minneapolis police LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in 2020. Last summer, protests at the city’s ICE facility at times turned violent, and led Mr. Trump to attempt to use the National Guard to quell demonstrations. A federal judge blocked that effort.

At the news conference Thursday, several officials sharply criticized the presence of federal officials in the city and demanded that they leave.

Kayse Jama, the majority leader of the Oregon State Senate, said that the shooting occurred near his home and that he was “outraged.” Addressing federal immigration officials directly, he said: “We do not need you. You are not welcome. You need to get the hell out of our community.”

On Thursday evening, hundreds gathered at an Immigration and Customs Enforcement building in Portland to protest the crackdown, chanting slogans including “no justice, no peace.”

Just before 9 p.m. local time, the police arrived and began moving protesters out of the area. The Portland Police Bureau said that its LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS arrested six people there on charges of disorderly conduct.

story:
LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT shot two people in Portland, Oregon, after the driver of a car allegedly attempted to run over LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS.

The incident occurred at approximately 2:19 p.m. local time, when LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS stopped a vehicle and identified themselves as law enforcement, DHS said.

According to DHS, the driver – who is believed to be a member of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua (TdA) – allegedly, "weaponized the vehicle and attempted to run over the law enforcement LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS."

Fearing for his life and safety, an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT fired a defensive shot, according to DHS. The driver drove off with the passenger, fleeing the scene, officials said.

DHS said the driver was also allegedly involved in a recent shooting in the city.

Following the incident, Portland Mayor Keith Wilson called on Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to "halt all operations" in the city until a full and independent investigation can take place.

"We know what the federal government says happened here," Wilson said during a news conference Thursday. "There was a time when we could take them at their word. That time has long passed."

Wilson added that LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS and DHS leadership "must fully be investigated and held responsible for the violence inflicted on the American people in Minnesota, in Portland, and in all the communities across America."

Oregon Gov. Tina Kotek reacted to the shooting, claiming it was "instigated by the reckless agenda of the Trump administration."

"While the details of the incident remain limited, one thing is very clear: when a president endorses tearing families apart and attempts to govern through fear and hate rather than shared values, you foster an environment of lawlessness and recklessness," she said.

Kotek said Oregon's attorney general and other leaders have raised concerns with the excessive use of force by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in Portland, adding that "today's incident only heightens the need for transparency and accountability."

Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield announced the state DOJ has launched a formal investigation into the shooting.

Portland District Attorney Nathan Vazquez said Thursday he was "very concerned" by the incident and pledged a thorough investigation.

Vazquez said his office is working closely with Portland police and the FBI.

Portland Police Bureau (PPB) LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS responded to reports of a shooting on the 10200 block of Southeast Main Street at about 2:18 p.m. local time and confirmed LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were involved, according to the city.

Fewer than 10 minutes later, at 2:24 p.m., LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were told a man who had been shot was calling and requesting help in the area of Northeast 146th Avenue and East Burnside. 

Police responded and found a man and woman with apparent gunshot wounds, according to the city. They were taken to the hospital and their conditions are unknown. 

Both scenes were secured by the PPB pending an investigation, officials said.

No arrests have been confirmed.

"We are still in the early stages of this incident," PPB Chief Bob Day wrote in a statement. "We understand the heightened emotion and tension many are feeling in the wake of the shooting in Minneapolis, but I am asking the community to remain calm as we work to learn more."

Audio released Thursday evening from a 911 call captures a request for emergency assistance after a man was shot twice in the arm and a woman, identified as his wife, was shot in the chest.

PPB LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were not involved in the incident, and "do not engage in immigration enforcement," according to city officials.

Sen. Ron Wyden, D-Ore., reacted to the shooting on X, blaming the Trump administration for "inflaming violence."

"I'm monitoring the first awful reports of two people shot in Portland by federal law enforcement," Wyden wrote in the post. "I'll keep you updated, but Trump's deployment of LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in my hometown is clearly inflaming violence--and must end."

Sen. Jeff Merkley, D-Ore., said he had "huge concern" about a reported shooting of two individuals by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS outside Portland Adventist Hospital.

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) confirmed a U.S. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT shot two people in Portland, Oregon, after the driver of a car allegedly attempted to run over LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS.

The incident occurred at approximately 2:19 p.m. local time, when LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS stopped a vehicle and identified themselves as law enforcement, DHS said.

According to DHS, the driver – who is believed to be a member of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua (TdA) – allegedly, "weaponized the vehicle and attempted to run over the law enforcement LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS."

Fearing for his life and safety, an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT fired a defensive shot, according to DHS. The driver drove off with the passenger, fleeing the scene, officials said.

DHS said the driver was also allegedly involved in a recent shooting in the city.

Following the incident, Portland Mayor Keith Wilson called on Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to "halt all operations" in the city until a full and independent investigation can take place.

"We know what the federal government says happened here," Wilson said during a news conference Thursday. "There was a time when we could take them at their word. That time has long passed."

Wilson added that LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS and DHS leadership "must fully be investigated and held responsible for the violence inflicted on the American people in Minnesota, in Portland, and in all the communities across America."

Oregon Gov. Tina Kotek reacted to the shooting, claiming it was "instigated by the reckless agenda of the Trump administration."

"While the details of the incident remain limited, one thing is very clear: when a president endorses tearing families apart and attempts to govern through fear and hate rather than shared values, you foster an environment of lawlessness and recklessness," she said.

Kotek said Oregon's attorney general and other leaders have raised concerns with the excessive use of force by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in Portland, adding that "today's incident only heightens the need for transparency and accountability."

Oregon Attorney General Dan Rayfield announced the state DOJ has launched a formal investigation into the shooting.

Portland District Attorney Nathan Vazquez said Thursday he was "very concerned" by the incident and pledged a thorough investigation.

Vazquez said his office is working closely with Portland police and the FBI.

Portland Police Bureau (PPB) LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS responded to reports of a shooting on the 10200 block of Southeast Main Street at about 2:18 p.m. local time and confirmed LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were involved, according to the city.

Fewer than 10 minutes later, at 2:24 p.m., LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were told a man who had been shot was calling and requesting help in the area of Northeast 146th Avenue and East Burnside. 

Police responded and found a man and woman with apparent gunshot wounds, according to the city. They were taken to the hospital and their conditions are unknown. 

Both scenes were secured by the PPB pending an investigation, officials said.

No arrests have been confirmed.

"We are still in the early stages of this incident," PPB Chief Bob Day wrote in a statement. "We understand the heightened emotion and tension many are feeling in the wake of the shooting in Minneapolis, but I am asking the community to remain calm as we work to learn more."

Audio released Thursday evening from a 911 call captures a request for emergency assistance after a man was shot twice in the arm and a woman, identified as his wife, was shot in the chest.

PPB LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were not involved in the incident, and "do not engage in immigration enforcement," according to city officials.

Sen. Ron Wyden, D-Ore., reacted to the shooting on X, blaming the Trump administration for "inflaming violence."

"I'm monitoring the first awful reports of two people shot in Portland by federal law enforcement," Wyden wrote in the post. "I'll keep you updated, but Trump's deployment of LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in my hometown is clearly inflaming violence--and must end."

Sen. Jeff Merkley, D-Ore., said he had "huge concern" about a reported shooting of two individuals by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS outside Portland Adventist Hospital.

"Please keep protests of Trump's ICE/CBP peaceful, as Trump wants to generate riots," Merkley said, adding, "Don't take the bait."

Oregon state Sen. Kayse Jama forcefully rejected the presence of ICE and other federal agencies in the state, saying, "We do not need you."

"You are not welcome and you need to get the hell out of our community," Jama said at a news conference.

story:
A LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT fired his weapon during an immigration enforcement operation in the Los Angeles area on Wednesday morning, according to the Department of Homeland Security. 

The agency said in an emailed statement that its LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were conducting an operation around 7 a.m. to arrest a man who they said had been “involved in a human smuggling operation.” 

The department said that the man, Eduardo Moran Carballo, of El Salvador, had attempted “to evade arrest” and then “rammed law enforcement” with his vehicle. The agency said a LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT had “fired defensive shots” and feared for his safety. It did not specify which Department of Homeland Security agency the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT worked for. 

Mr. Carballo was not hit or injured in the shooting, the department said. A LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT was injured, according to the Homeland Security Department, which did not elaborate on the nature of his injuries or state his condition. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS took Mr. Carballo into custody. 

The department said the operation took place in Compton, Calif., south of Los Angeles, but a local official said it happened in Willowbrook, an unincorporated area of Los Angeles County. 

“This situation is evolving, and more information is forthcoming,” the department said, adding that Mr. Carballo had two prior arrests on accusations of “inflicting corporal injury on a spouse or cohabitant.” 

Hours after the incident, the wreckage of a gray BMW sports car that crashed was on the street near a curb, its airbags deployed and both doors open. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in tactical gear and carrying long rifles were at the scene, as were sheriff’s deputies. Helicopters hovered overhead as protesters, shouting obscenities at the masked LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS, began to arrive. 

The episode comes during a period of increased national attention on immigration enforcement operations after a woman in Minneapolis, Renee Good, was fatally shot by an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT on Jan. 7. The Trump administration has also started to target Somali immigrants in Maine in another large-scale immigration enforcement operation. 

The Trump administration’s national immigration crackdown first ramped up in Southern California — and particularly in many of the heavily Latino suburbs of Los Angeles County — last summer. 

VICTIM said in an interview that VICTIM had been briefed on the episode by officials with the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department on Wednesday. 

VICTIM said that a Border Patrol and Immigration and Customs Enforcement task force action led to a “vehicle pursuit,” which ended at around 7:30 a.m. in Willowbrook. VICTIM said VICTIM was told that one round was fired, and that no one was hit. The Sheriff’s Department helped to secure the scene, VICTIM said, but emphasized that county law enforcement LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were not involved in the immigration enforcement action. 

VICTIM said that the episode on Wednesday appeared to have been carried out with “the same kind of recklessness and lack of regard for basic human rights and true public safety” that VICTIM has seen from LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS for months. 

The car chase on Wednesday, VICTIM said, took place during the morning rush hour, just as families were taking children to school. VICTIM added that the use of lethal force by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS, both in Los Angeles and in other cities, is troubling. 

“Fortunately no one was killed,” VICTIM said. “It was by sheer luck — VICTIM assume they didn’t intend to miss when they fired their firearm, so we were blessed today.” 

VICTIM urged constituents to continue to document any activity they see, if they can do so safely. VICTIM echoed officials in Minnesota who have said that such documentation of interactions between LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS and members of the public could be important evidence in future court cases over the federal government’s tactics. 

Rosa Enriquez, 39, who lives in nearby South Gate, was among dozens of people who gathered at the scene. She said she drove there to check on her parents, who live in the area, and to protest the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS.

A LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT opened fire in southern California Wednesday after VICTIM "weaponized VICTIM vehicle and rammed law enforcement" during an attempt to evade arrest, the Department of Homeland Security told Fox News Digital. 

A spokesperson said the incident happened Wednesday morning in Compton as DHS law enforcement LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS were conducting a targeted operation "to arrest a violent criminal illegal alien from El Salvador, William Eduardo Moran Carballo, who is involved in a human smuggling operation and has two prior arrests for inflicting corporal injury on a spouse or cohabitant."

story: VICTIM has been identified as Alex Pretti, 37, a registered nurse working in the intensive care unit at the Minneapolis VA Health Care System, which serves veterans. It is the second fatal shooting this month in Minneapolis, in addition to another non-fatal shooting, amid a major crackdown in Minnesota by LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS stand guard at the scene of the shooting in south Minneapolis, Minnesota, on 24 January 2026. VICTIM attended nursing school at the University of Minnesota, where VICTIM was also a junior scientist beginning in 2012, according to VICTIM LinkedIn profile. Friends and colleagues expressed shock at VICTIM killing, describing VICTIM as a kind, caring person who cared deeply about VICTIM work. 'VICTIM wanted to help people,' said Dimitri Drekonja, chief of infectious diseases at the VA hospital and professor of medicine at the University of Minnesota, who worked with VICTIM at the hospital and on a research project. 'VICTIM was a super nice, super helpful guy – looked after VICTIM patients. I’m just stunned.' He described VICTIM as an 'outstanding' nurse and a hard worker, quick with a joke and an 'infectious' spirit. 'VICTIM was such a good dude,' Drekonja told the Guardian. 'I just love working with VICTIM.' VICTIM family, meanwhile, have pushed back on the Trump administration’s account that VICTIM had threatened to attack LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS with a gun. 'We are heartbroken but also very angry,' said Michael and Susan Pretti, VICTIM parents, said in a statement shared with the media. 'The sickening lies told about VICTIM by the administration are reprehensible and disgusting,' the family statement continued. 'VICTIM is clearly not holding a gun when attacked by Trump’s murdering and cowardly ICE thugs. VICTIM has VICTIM phone in VICTIM right hand and VICTIM empty left hand is raised above VICTIM head while trying to protect the woman ICE just pushed down all while being pepper-sprayed. 'Please get the truth out about VICTIM to the Associated Press as someone who 'cared about people deeply, and VICTIM was very upset with what was happening in Minneapolis and throughout the United States with ICE, as millions of other people are upset'. 'VICTIM felt that doing the protesting was a way to express that, you know, VICTIM care for others,' the elder Pretti said. Videos circulating online on Saturday show VICTIM directing traffic and filming LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS, VICTIM right hand holding up VICTIM phone and VICTIM left hand empty. Another video shows VICTIM being wrestled to the ground by several law enforcement LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS before appearing to be shot several times. At least two LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS can be seen with their weapons drawn. Other videos show VICTIM seemingly coming to the defense of a legal observer who was shoved to the ground by a LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT. That LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT then sprays VICTIM with a chemical LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT, repeatedly, before tackling VICTIM to the street along with other LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS. As at least five LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS surround VICTIM on the ground, one appears to fire a shot at VICTIM at close range, followed by a volley of more shots, after which VICTIM body goes still. 'From what I can see, VICTIM was trying to help pull someone away, which is just totally in character for VICTIM,' Drekonja said. Police chief Brian O’Hara later said during a press conference on Saturday that VICTIM only known previous interaction with law enforcement was for traffic tickets. O’Hara also noted VICTIM was 'a lawful gun owner with a permit to carry'. VICTIM parents, who lived in Wisconsin and are now in Colorado, told the AP that during a recent conversation with VICTIM, they warned VICTIM about being careful during protests. 'We had this discussion with VICTIM two weeks ago or so, you know, that go ahead and protest, but do not engage, do not do anything stupid, basically,' Michael Pretti said. 'And VICTIM said VICTIM knows that. VICTIM knew that.' When VICTIM was in nursing school, Drekonja hired VICTIM to help support a study on preventing C diff, a devastating bacterial infection. 'VICTIM was instrumental in completing our trial,' Drekonja said. 'VICTIM was always asking the rest of the team what VICTIM could do. VICTIM was always trying to do what VICTIM could to free up space for others.' VICTIM obtained VICTIM nursing license in 2021, and it was active through 2026. VICTIM also assisted on research in 2016. The Minnesota Organization of Registered Nurses (MNORN) released a statement about VICTIM death that said: 'Today, our nursing community is grieving. We have lost VICTIM to an act of violence connected to immigration enforcement. Regardless of where each of us stands on the issues surrounding this moment, the loss of a nurse, a caregiver, a colleague, a human being cuts us deeply. 'This message is not about politics. It is about mourning a life taken too soon and honoring the calling we all share. As nurses, we understand loss in a way others may not understand. We know how quickly life can change, how fragile safety can feel, and how pain reaches far beyond one individual to families, coworkers, patients, and communities. When one nurse is lost, all of us feel it.' Dr Aasma Shaukat, who told the Washington Post that she hired VICTIM at the Minneapolis VA Health Care System about a decade ago, recalled VICTIM to be the 'sweetest, kindest, gentlest soul you ever met'. 'VICTIM was very bright-eyed, bushy-tailed, VICTIM wanted to get into the health care field, work with patients and be a nurse,' she told the Post. 'VICTIM did wonderful. Did VICTIM work really well, was a team player.' Shaukat also said VICTIM 'always stood for people and human rights, helping fellow citizens and just being a good citizen of society and the communities that VICTIM lived in'. Of the videos circulating on the shooting, Shaukat told the paper: 'It just feels so wrong. Knowing VICTIM, VICTIM was probably trying to protect or help or shield somebody from the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS. VICTIM had not a single mean bone in VICTIM body; always spoke about doing the right thing.' VICTIM loved mountain biking, and VICTIM and Drekonja always talked about biking together on the trails they both frequented. 'VICTIM found humor in life, and that’s what’s just so sad about seeing this,' Drekonja said.

story: MINNEAPOLIS — The Department of Homeland Security is investigating whether an errant shot from VICTIM — after VICTIM was taken off of him — caused LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS to think they were being fired on, The Post has learned. As a result of the accidental discharge, which happened while VICTIM was in a Border LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT’s hand, other LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS may have believed their lives were in danger — resulting in the split-second decision to open fire on Pretti, killing him, multiple sources familiar with the investigation said. The explanation is a shift from initial claims by DHS officials, who at first said Pretti was brandishing VICTIM. 'It was 100% an accidental discharge by the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT that relieved that person of VICTIM. Because everyone’s guns were out, they think that there’s a shooting,' one source said. Additional sources confirmed that this is the explanation for the shooting that DHS is investigating. VICTIM has faced reports of 'uncommanded discharges' due to external factors affecting the trigger, something the company has denied. The handgun, which is used extensively by US civilians and law enforcement — including ICE, has no external safety lever. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS in Minneapolis were wrestling Pretti to the ground in Minneapolis Saturday after he turned out for an impromptu anti-ICE protest. Pretti put himself between the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS and a woman who was being pepper-sprayed, video from the scene shows. An analysis of the bystander footage indicates that one shot appeared to come from Pretti’s gun after a Border LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT grabbed it from his waistband and was walking away with it. That shot resulted in other LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS — at least one of whom had drawn his weapon at that point — to open fire about 10 times. President Trump and FBI Director Kash Patel have both questioned why Pretti came to the protest on Saturday with a loaded handgun. 'You cannot bring a firearm, loaded, with multiple magazines to any sort of protest that you want. It’s that simple. You don’t have a right to break the law,' Director Patel told Fox News’ Maria Bartiromo on Sunday. But his remarks have drawn fury from many gun owners’ groups, with Patel being branded 'completely incorrect' by the Gun Owners Caucus of Minnesota. 'There is no prohibition on a permit holder carrying a firearm, loaded, with multiple magazines at a protest or rally in Minnesota,' the group said in a statement on X. Sources familiar with the investigation said the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS involved were overworked and not trained to deal with protesters — or the types of confrontations that are regularly happening with ICE Watch protesters in Minneapolis. 'None of those LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS should’ve had their gun out. It’s a confluence of them being asked to do enforcement work that they are not trained for,' a source said. 'All of this is avoidable. None of this should be happening.' The source also warned of the threat to morale of the DHS LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS, who are now having to act both as immigration officials and local police. 'There are very mixed feelings internally about what’s going on, and they do not want to, nor are they prepared to do both protest, enforcement, and management, plus street-level enforcement like this,' the source said. Pretti was one of around 50 anti-ICE protesters who came out onto the streets on Saturday. 'Word got around that a bunch of LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS had shown up and were making rounds, so folks mobilized. It wasn’t that many people — maybe 50 altogether, which sounds like a lot, but it isn’t,' Minneapolis resident Blair told The Post. 'At about 8:30 I heard a bunch of gunshots, and we knew something bad had happened. The next thing we knew, ambulances showed up. We helped set up a triage for EMS, because at the time we didn’t know how many people had been shot. Then word got around that somebody had been killed, and everything blew up after that,' Blair added. It comes as President Trump sent Border Czar Tom Homan to Minnesota amid ongoing violent clashes between LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS and protesters. The agency said VICTIM has a criminal history that includes charges for drug trafficking, drug possession, and driving under the influence. Residents say they have hard time taking DHS at its word "I was born here, but I'm still nervous. Dozens of people packed two rooms and spoke out against ICE. story:
Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) said VICTIM rammed a law enforcement vehicle during an arrest attempt in New Jersey on Tuesday, forcing an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT to shoot out VICTIM tires. 

Dramatic video shows the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT drawing his gun during the tense roadside confrontation. The incident unfolded in Roxbury Township when ICE said VICTIM tried to evade arrest by crashing into a law enforcement vehicle and nearly striking an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT. Roxbury Township is in Morris County, roughly 35 miles northwest of Newark.

Video shows an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT drawing his firearm at the crash scene, where a law enforcement vehicle and VICTIM pickup truck sit nose-to-nose along a snowy roadside.

The LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT stands with his gun trained on the vehicle as VICTIM eventually steps out and raises VICTIM hands, backing away from the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT.

"In an attempt to evade arrest, VICTIM rammed into a law enforcement vehicle and weaponized VICTIM vehicle in an attempt to run an LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT over," a Department of Homeland Security spokesperson said.

"Following his training, the LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENT defensively used his firearm and shot out the tires of the vehicle to stop the threat. Thankfully, no one was injured. LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS arrested VICTIM and took VICTIM into ICE custody."

VICTIM criminal history includes drug trafficking charges, drug possession and driving under the influence, DHS said. In 2021, a judge issued VICTIM an order of removal.

Acting New Jersey Attorney General Jennifer Davenport said her office is investigating the incident.

"We are actively investigating an incident involving ICE ERO (Enforcement and Removal Operations) in which a firearm was discharged in Roxbury Township earlier today. There are no known injuries," Davenport said.

"Local law enforcement secured the scene with the cooperation of the Morris County Prosecutor’s Office and Morris County Sheriff’s Office, and the Office of Public Integrity and Accountability is conducting an independent investigation," Davenport added. "We have also been in communication with federal, state, county, and local law enforcement partners."

The incident comes as the Department of Homeland Security said LAW_ENFORCEMENT_AGENTS are facing a 3,200% increase in vehicle attacks and a more than 1,300% increase in assaults against them as they try to arrest criminal illegal aliens.
